title: Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?
subtitle: This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.
caption:
Which countries include mumps vaccines in their national vaccination programs?

This shows which countries provide and recommend mumps vaccines through routine services. People may still be able to receive the vaccine if it's not in the routine schedule – it might be optional or available commercially.

This choropleth world map displays the status of mumps vaccine inclusion in national vaccination programs for 2024. The legend distinguishes between four categories: "Entire country" (blue), "Not routinely administered" (red), "Regions of the country" (orange), and "No data" (hatched). The blue color, indicating routine administration across the entire country, covers the majority of the map, including North America, South America, Europe, Russia, China, Australia, and Japan. In contrast, the red color, signifying that the vaccine is not routinely administered, dominates the African continent, the Middle East, and South Asia, including India. Specific countries in Southeast Asia, such as Indonesia and the Philippines, also appear in red. Greenland is the only large landmass shown with diagonal hatching, indicating no data is available. The orange category for partial regional coverage is not visibly present on the map. The visual distribution shows a clear divide where high-income regions and large parts of the Americas and East Asia have integrated the vaccine into routine schedules, while large portions of Africa and Asia have not. Improved mumps vaccines became available for the first time in 1967, providing the historical context for the development of these national programs.
